name: Scout APM MCP Server
what_it_does: Official MCP server from ScoutAPM (scoutapp org) for Scout APM — the developer-friendly application performance monitoring service. Enables AI agents to query Scout APM for transaction traces, performance bottlenecks, slow queries, memory/CPU metrics, and deployment tracking — supporting AI-driven performance analysis workflows.
use_cases: Querying Scout APM for performance bottlenecks and slow endpoints from optimization agents
Analyzing transaction traces and N+1 query problems from AI code review agents
Monitoring deployment performance impact via Scout APM from DevOps agents
Investigating memory and CPU performance issues using Scout data in AI troubleshooting workflows
not_for: Teams not using Scout APM (Datadog, New Relic, or other APM users)
Infrastructure monitoring (Scout focuses on application performance, not server metrics)
best_when: A developer using Scout APM for application performance monitoring wants AI agents to analyze performance data, identify bottlenecks, and assist with optimization decisions.
avoid_when: You use Datadog, New Relic, or other APM tools. Scout is particularly strong for Rails/Django apps.

auth.notes: Scout APM API key required. Available from Scout APM account settings.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Scout APM free tier: 1 app, 7-day retention
pricing.paid_tiers: Paid plans for more apps, longer retention, and team features
pricing.notes: Scout APM has a free tier. Paid plans for production use. Official MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: Scout API rate limits apply — generally adequate for analysis workflows

known_agent_gotchas: Scout APM data retention varies by plan — older performance data may not be available on free tier
Performance data is application-specific — agents need to know app names and endpoints
Official from scoutapp org — actively maintained by Scout APM team
N+1 query detection is Scout's strength — useful for AI-driven code optimization
agent_readiness.security_notes: APM data contains application behavior and potentially sensitive request parameters. API key required. HTTPS. Scout is US-based.

security_score_components.security_notes: APM data may include request parameters — treat as sensitive. Official implementation. HTTPS. API key auth.
